terms.title: Appliqué
terms.description: A decorative technique where pieces of fabric are sewn onto another fabric to create patterns or designs

terms.title: Back Stitch
terms.description: A strong hand stitch where each stitch goes slightly backward, creating a solid, continuous line often used for seams

terms.title: Back Tack
terms.description: Sewing backward for a few stitches to lock the thread and prevent seams from coming undone

terms.title: Bias Binding
terms.description: Strips of fabric cut diagonally, used to neatly finish edges and curves

terms.title: Bobbin
terms.description: A small spool under the needle plate that holds the lower thread in a sewing machine

terms.title: Grain
terms.description: The direction of threads in fabric, including lengthwise and crosswise lines

terms.title: Interfacing
terms.description: An extra layer of fabric added inside to give shape and support to garments

terms.title: Presser Foot
terms.description: The part of the sewing machine that holds the fabric in place while stitching

terms.title: Raw Edge
terms.description: The unfinished edge of fabric that can fray if not sewn or finished

terms.title: Reverse Stitch
terms.description: A machine function that sews backward to secure stitches at the beginning or end

terms.title: Right Side
terms.description: The outer, visible side of the fabric when the project is finished

terms.title: Seam Allowance
terms.description: The extra fabric between the stitching line and the edge, used to join pieces together

terms.title: Self-Lined Fabric
terms.description: Using the same fabric on both the inside and outside of a garment

terms.title: Selvedge
terms.description: The finished edge of fabric that doesn’t fray and runs along its length

terms.title: Stay Stitch
terms.description: A line of stitching sewn close to the edge to keep fabric from stretching out of shape

terms.title: Tacking Stitch
terms.description: Temporary hand stitches used to hold fabric pieces in place before final sewing

terms.title: Top Stitch
terms.description: A visible line of stitching on the outside of a garment, often used for decoration or strength

terms.title: Warp
terms.description: The threads that run lengthwise in fabric and stay fixed on the loom

terms.title: Weft
terms.description: The threads woven across the fabric, going over and under the warp threads

terms.title: Wrong Side
terms.description: The inside or hidden side of the fabric

terms.title: Zig-Zag Stitch
terms.description: A stitch that moves side to side, used to prevent fraying or add decoration

terms.title: Basting
terms.description: Long temporary stitches used to hold fabric layers together before final sewing, usually removed later

terms.title: Clip / Notch
terms.description: Small cuts made into seam allowance to help curves and corners lie flat after turning

terms.title: Dart
terms.description: A fold sewn into fabric to shape it around curves of the body

terms.title: Ease
terms.description: The slight fullness added to fabric so it fits comfortably without being tight

terms.title: Facing
terms.description: A piece of fabric used to finish raw edges, usually inside necklines or armholes

terms.title: Fold Line
terms.description: A marking on a pattern indicating the edge should be placed on a fabric fold

terms.title: Gathering
terms.description: Pulling fabric together using stitches to create fullness or ruffles

terms.title: Hem
terms.description: The finished edge of a garment, usually folded and sewn to prevent fraying

terms.title: Lining
terms.description: An inner layer of fabric that provides a clean finish and improves comfort

terms.title: Muslin (Toile)
terms.description: A test version of a garment made with inexpensive fabric to check fit before final sewing

terms.title: Notches
terms.description: Small marks on pattern pieces used to align fabric pieces correctly

terms.title: Overlock / Serger
terms.description: A type of stitch or machine that trims and finishes edges at the same time

terms.title: Pattern Piece
terms.description: A template used to cut fabric into the correct shapes for a garment

terms.title: Pleat
terms.description: A fold in fabric that is stitched or pressed into place for shape or style

terms.title: Pressing
terms.description: Using an iron to flatten seams and shape fabric during sewing, not the same as ironing clothes

terms.title: Seam Finish
terms.description: Techniques used to prevent raw edges from fraying, such as zig-zag or overlock

terms.title: Seam Ripper
terms.description: A small tool used to remove stitches without damaging fabric

terms.title: Slip Stitch
terms.description: A nearly invisible hand stitch used to close seams or hems from the outside

terms.title: Stay Tape
terms.description: A strip of material used to stabilize seams and prevent stretching

terms.title: Understitch
terms.description: A line of stitching that keeps facing or lining from rolling to the outside

terms.title: Walking Foot
terms.description: A sewing machine foot that feeds multiple layers evenly, useful for thick fabrics or quilting

terms.title: Nap
terms.description: The texture direction of fabrics like velvet or corduroy that affects appearance when cut

terms.title: Bias
terms.description: The diagonal direction of fabric that stretches more than straight grain

terms.title: Cut on Fold
terms.description: A pattern instruction where fabric is folded to create a symmetrical piece

terms.title: Grading
terms.description: Trimming seam allowances to different widths to reduce bulk

terms.title: Layering
terms.description: Stacking seam allowances at different lengths to create smoother edges

terms.title: Twin Needle
terms.description: A sewing machine needle with two points used for parallel stitching lines

terms.title: Feed Dogs
terms.description: The metal teeth under the presser foot that move fabric through the machine

terms.title: Thread Tension
terms.description: The balance of upper and lower threads that affects stitch quality

terms.title: Lock Stitch
terms.description: A basic stitch formed by interlocking upper and bobbin threads

terms.title: Armscye
terms.description: The armhole opening in a garment where the sleeve is attached

terms.title: Bishop Sleeve
terms.description: A long sleeve that is full and gathered at the wrist

terms.title: Boning
terms.description: Rigid strips inserted into garments like corsets to provide structure and support

terms.title: Box Pleat
terms.description: A pleat made by folding fabric in opposite directions to create a box shape

terms.title: Bust Dart
terms.description: A dart specifically placed to shape fabric around the bust

terms.title: Casing
terms.description: A fabric channel used to hold elastic or drawstring

terms.title: Continuous Binding
terms.description: A method of finishing edges using one long strip of bias binding

terms.title: Crotch Curve
terms.description: The curved seam in pants that shapes the fit between the legs

terms.title: Drop Shoulder
terms.description: A sleeve style where the shoulder seam falls below the natural shoulder line

terms.title: Edge Stitch
terms.description: A line of stitching sewn very close to the edge for a clean finish

terms.title: French Seam
terms.description: A seam that encloses raw edges inside, creating a clean finish inside garments

terms.title: Godet
terms.description: A triangular piece of fabric inserted into a garment to add flare and movement

terms.title: Gusset
terms.description: An extra piece of fabric added to provide ease of movement or reduce stress on seams

terms.title: Inseam
terms.description: The seam running along the inside of the leg in pants

terms.title: Interlining
terms.description: A layer of fabric between the outer fabric and lining for extra warmth or structure

terms.title: Keyhole Opening
terms.description: A small decorative opening in a garment, often at the neckline

terms.title: Lapped Zipper
terms.description: A zipper insertion method where one side overlaps the zipper teeth

terms.title: Mandarin Collar
terms.description: A short, stand-up collar without a fold-over

terms.title: Mock Seam
terms.description: A decorative seam that looks functional but does not join fabric pieces

terms.title: Princess Seam
terms.description: A long curved seam that shapes a garment closely to the body

terms.title: Raglan Sleeve
terms.description: A sleeve that extends in one piece to the collar, without a shoulder seam

terms.title: Rolled Hem
terms.description: A very narrow hem rolled tightly and stitched, often used on lightweight fabrics

terms.title: Scye Depth
terms.description: The vertical measurement of an armhole, important for fit

terms.title: Set-in Sleeve
terms.description: A sleeve sewn into a separate armhole seam

terms.title: Shirring
terms.description: Gathering fabric using elastic thread to create stretch and texture

terms.title: Sloper
terms.description: A basic pattern used as a foundation to create other designs

terms.title: Tuck
terms.description: A fold sewn into fabric for decorative or shaping purposes

terms.title: Vent
terms.description: A slit in a garment that allows ease of movement, often at hems

terms.title: Yoke
terms.description: A fitted section of a garment, usually at the shoulders or waist, that supports the rest

terms.title: Quilting
terms.description: Sewing layers of fabric together with padding in between to create texture and warmth

terms.title: Batting
terms.description: The soft layer placed between fabric layers in quilting

terms.title: Chain Stitch
terms.description: A stitch that forms loops like a chain, often used in decorative sewing

terms.title: Cover Stitch
terms.description: A professional stitch used for hemming knit fabrics, seen in T-shirts

terms.title: Overcasting
terms.description: Stitching over the edge of fabric to prevent fraying

terms.title: Seam Line
terms.description: The actual line where stitches are sewn, distinct from the seam allowance

terms.title: Pattern Markings
terms.description: Symbols and lines on patterns that guide cutting and sewing, such as darts, grainlines, and notches

terms.title: Grainline
terms.description: A line on a pattern piece that shows how it should align with the fabric grain

terms.title: Bust Point
terms.description: The fullest point of the bust used as a reference for fitting garments

terms.title: Notch Matching
terms.description: Aligning notches on pattern pieces to ensure correct assembly

terms.title: Underlining
terms.description: A layer of fabric sewn to the main fabric to add structure and improve finish

terms.title: Interfacing Types
terms.description: Different forms of interfacing such as fusible, sew-in, knit, or woven used for specific purposes

terms.title: Fabric Stretch
terms.description: The ability of fabric to stretch in one or more directions

terms.title: Fabric Weight
terms.description: The heaviness or thickness of fabric, affecting drape and usage

terms.title: Drape
terms.description: How fabric hangs and flows when worn or used

terms.title: Seam Slippage
terms.description: When fabric pulls away from stitches, causing gaps in seams

terms.title: Puckering
terms.description: Wrinkling or gathering of fabric caused by incorrect tension or stitching

terms.title: Skipped Stitches
terms.description: Missing stitches caused by needle or machine issues

terms.title: Thread Bunching
terms.description: Tangled thread buildup, often under the fabric, due to tension or threading problems

terms.title: Needle Types
terms.description: Different sewing machine needles such as universal, ballpoint, denim, or stretch needles

terms.title: Needle Size
terms.description: The thickness of a needle, selected based on fabric type and thread

terms.title: Hand Sewing Needle
terms.description: A needle used for manual stitching, varying in size and purpose

terms.title: Rotary Cutter
terms.description: A cutting tool with a circular blade used for precise fabric cutting

terms.title: Cutting Mat
terms.description: A protective surface used with rotary cutters to prevent damage to work surfaces

terms.title: Tailor’s Chalk
terms.description: A marking tool used to draw temporary lines on fabric

terms.title: Seam Gauge
terms.description: A small ruler used to measure seam allowances and hems accurately

terms.title: Dress Form
terms.description: A mannequin used for fitting and designing garments

terms.title: Buttonhole
terms.description: A stitched opening that allows a button to pass through

terms.title: Button Placket
terms.description: The strip of fabric where buttons and buttonholes are placed

terms.title: Elastic
terms.description: A stretchy material used to gather fabric or create fitted areas

terms.title: Drawstring
terms.description: A cord or ribbon used to tighten fabric, often inside a casing

terms.title: Bias Cut
terms.description: Cutting fabric diagonally to allow more stretch and fluid drape

terms.title: Grain Distortion
terms.description: When fabric grain becomes misaligned due to handling or cutting
